mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 1 Series 118i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B38 three‑cylinder typically costs £3,000–£5,000 supply only, with older N46/N43/N13 units slightly lower. Fitting adds £800–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 118i.

It depends which engine your 118i has. The N46 is the most reliable of the early units. The N43 and N13 are powerful but have timing chain and HPFP watchpoints. The B38 is the most reliable overall with only cooling system concerns. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 118i engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch (N43/N13), HPFP failure (N43/N13), carbon buildup (N13), and water pump failure (B38). Each generation has its own character and watchpoints.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 118i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. An E87 118i is a £1,000–£4,000 vehicle — replacement may exceed our 70% BMW scrap threshold on lower-value examples. An F40 118i is a £10,000–£20,000 vehicle — a £3,500–£4,500 recon is 18–45% of the car's value, making replacement viable. Evaluate carefully based on your car's generation and condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 118i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 118i covers N46 (2004–2007), N43 (2007–2011), N13 (2011–2019), and B38 (2019+). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the timing chain issues on a 118i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — regular oil changes with BMW Longlife‑04 spec oil and shortened intervals (5,000–7,500 miles) are the most effective prevention for N43/N13 engines. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (N43/N13, E81/E87 2007–2011 & F20/F21 2011–2019)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (chain and tensioner replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before catastrophic failure)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: This is the defining issue for N43 and N13 118is. The timing chain tensioner can fail, allowing the chain to stretch or skip. Regular oil changes with BMW Longlife‑04 spec oil are essential. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, have the chain inspected immediately — catching it early saves you from a £1,800+ repair bill.

problems.title: High‑Pressure Fuel Pump Failure (N43/N13, E81/E87 2007–2011 & F20/F21 2011–2019)
problems.symptoms: Long crank on start, rough idle, loss of power, engine stalling, engine warning light.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless injectors also damaged)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: The HPFP is a known weak point on both N43 and N13 engines. If you experience long cranking times or rough running, have the fuel pressure tested. Replacing the HPFP is far cheaper than the collateral damage that can occur from injector failure if fuel pressure drops.

problems.title: Cooling System Issues (B38, F40/F70 2019+)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, temperature fluctuations, engine warning light, heater not working properly.
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (water pump or thermostat replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The B38's cooling system, like other modern BMW engines, can suffer from water pump and thermostat failures. The electric water pump is a common failure point. If you notice temperature fluctuations or coolant loss, have the system inspected. Replacing a failing water pump preventatively is cheaper than the overheating damage it can cause.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 118i? Check These First
buyIf: * N43/N13 (2007–2019): documented timing chain history (replaced or inspected)
* N13 (2011–2019): carbon cleaning performed — or budget for it
* B38 (2019+): cooling system maintained with recent water pump work
* Full service history with BMW-spec oil (Longlife‑04 or LL‑12 FE)
avoidIf: * Cold‑start rattle with no timing chain history on N43/N13 — walk away
* Long cranking on N43/N13 — HPFP may be failing
* Coolant loss with no diagnosis on B38 — water pump may be failing
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially expensive injection issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 118i badge is a fascinating journey through BMW petrol engine development — from naturally aspirated N46/N43 to the turbocharged N13 and the modern B38 three-cylinder. The N46 is the most reliable of the early units; the N43 and N13 are more powerful but have timing chain and HPFP watchpoints; the B38 is the most reliable overall with only cooling system concerns. Buy on generation-specific history — and never ignore a cold‑start rattle.

cta.label: → Compare 118i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 1 Series 118i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking; timing chain issue indicator (N43/N13)
* Cranking Time — Check for long cranking; HPFP failure indicator (N43/N13)
* Service History — including which engine era (N46/N43/N13/B38) and confirmed oil‑change intervals
* Coolant Level (B38) — Check for unexplained loss; water pump may be failing
* Carbon Build-up (N13) — Check for rough idle or hesitation; may need walnut blasting
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
